text:
be entitled to superannuation benefits. This is the only 
construction which would harmonise the two provisions. It 
is well-settled rule of construction that in case of apparent 
conflict between the two provisions, they should be so 
interpreted that the effect is given to both. Hence, we are of 
the opinion that such of the employees who are otherwise 
entitled to superannuation benefits under the Regulations 
if visited with the penalty of removal from service with 
superannuation benefits shall be entitled for those benefits 
and such of the employees though visited with the same 
penalty but are not eligible for superannuation benefits 
under the Regulations shall not be entitled to that.
22. Both the learned Single Judge and the Division Bench had followed 
the aforesaid decision of this Court. Learned Single Judge noted 
that respondent had submitted his option for pension on 05.10.2010.
